message:
Resilient indexing pipeline: checkpoint resume, embedding retries, degraded-mode completion, watcher auto-restart, and heap/normalization fixes

This commit introduces a comprehensive resilience overhaul across the
indexing, watching, search, and storage subsystems. The core motivation
is ensuring that transient failures (embedder unavailability, slow CI
workers, filesystem pressure) no longer cause hard crashes or silent
data loss, but instead degrade gracefully with full observability.

== Indexing Pipeline (runtime.rs) ==

- Add checkpoint-based resume: new IndexingCheckpoint and CheckpointFileEntry
  structs are serialized to index_checkpoint.json every 4 batches. On
  restart, already-indexed files are skipped, avoiding redundant work.
  The checkpoint is removed on successful non-degraded completion.

- Embedder probe retry loop: the single probe call is replaced with up to
  EMBEDDING_PROBE_MAX_RETRIES (2) attempts with exponential backoff
  (500ms, 1000ms). On exhaustion, the system falls back to hash
  embeddings and sets embedder_degraded=true rather than crashing.

- Embedding batch retry loop: each embed_batch call is retried up to
  EMBEDDING_BATCH_MAX_RETRIES (3) times with exponential backoff
  (200ms, 400ms, 800ms). Permanently failed batches defer their files
  for a later semantic upgrade pass instead of silently dropping vectors.

- Semantic upgrade pass: after the main indexing loop, if running in
  hash-fallback mode and files were deferred, the system re-probes the
  real embedder. If it is now available, it recreates the vector index
  with real embeddings, recovering from the degraded state within the
  same run when possible.

- New progress stages: RetryingEmbedding, SemanticUpgrade, and
  CompletedDegraded are added to IndexingProgressStage with labels,
  colors, and TUI rendering support. The progress snapshot now carries
  embedding_retries, embedding_failures, semantic_deferred_files,
  embedder_degraded, degradation_reason, and recent_warnings.

- Health panel in TUI: a new "health" panel renders in the indexing
  progress view when degradation, retries, or warnings are present,
  showing severity-tagged warning messages with capped history (max 8).

- Non-TUI progress output: the plain-text progress renderer also prints
  health status lines (DEGRADED/PARTIAL FAILURES/RETRIES OCCURRED) and
  contextual tip messages that adapt to degraded vs. normal completion.

- vector_writer wrapped in Option<>: allows the writer to be taken and
  recreated during semantic upgrade without use-after-move (fixes E0382
  compile error reported in issue bd-1p1v).

- Removed FRANKENSEARCH_CHECK_UPDATES / FSFS_CHECK_UPDATES env override
  from apply_cli_env_overrides and its test, cleaning up dead config.

== File Watcher (watcher.rs) ==

- Auto-restart with exponential backoff: the watcher worker thread now
  loops on run_worker_loop errors with up to MAX_RESTARTS (10) attempts,
  using backoff from 500ms to 30s (capped). Previously, any init error
  killed the watcher permanently.

- New stats fields: events_dropped_pressure and worker_restarts are added
  to WatcherStats/WatcherStatsInner, tracked via AtomicU64, giving
  operators visibility into pressure-induced event drops and restarts.

- Catchup tracking after pressure relief: when events are dropped during
  high-pressure periods (watching_enabled=false), the flag
  events_were_dropped is set. When pressure returns to normal, a debug
  log signals that a catchup scan should be scheduled on the next cycle.

== Durability (file_protector.rs) ==

- Split repair_file into repair_file + repair_file_internal: the new
  internal method accepts separate dest_path and source_path parameters.
  The public repair_file passes path for both (preserving API). The
  safe_repair_with_backup caller now passes the backup_path as
  source_path when a backup exists, ensuring repair reads from the
  backup copy rather than the potentially-corrupted destination.

== Search & Index (search.rs, mrl.rs) ==

- Bounded BinaryHeap pre-allocation: all BinaryHeap::with_capacity calls
  in scan_range, scan_range_filtered, scan_sequential (mrl.rs), and
  merge_partial_heaps now use limit.min(max_elements) instead of bare
  limit. This prevents pathological over-allocation when a caller
  requests limit=usize::MAX or limit >> actual record count, which
  could cause OOM on large indices.

== Score Normalization (blend.rs) ==

- Improved robust_normalize threshold curve: the blending threshold
  transition from identity-mapped to fully-normalized scores now uses a
  smoother ramp: t = ((range - 0.01) / 0.04).clamp(0.0, 1.0) instead
  of t = (range / 0.01).clamp(0.0, 1.0). This prevents a discontinuous
  jump at the threshold boundary that could cause score instability when
  fusing results from multiple retrieval sources with similar score
  ranges.

== Storage Pipeline (job_queue.rs, pipeline.rs) ==

- resurrect_terminal_failures: new method on PersistentJobQueue that
  resets all terminally-failed jobs for a given embedder_id back to
  pending status with retry_count=0. Intended for use on startup when
  the embedder configuration changes, giving previously impossible jobs
  another chance. Includes 3 unit tests covering: basic resurrection,
  embedder-scoped filtering, and no-op on empty queue.

- Terminal failure tracking in pipeline: handle_job_failure now returns
  bool indicating whether the failure was terminal. BatchProcessResult
  and WorkerReport gain terminal_failures / terminal_failures_encountered
  fields. The worker exit log now includes the terminal failure count.
  Test fixtures updated to include the new fields.

== E2E Tests (e2e_recall.rs) ==

- Timeout budget increase: FSFS_PROCESS_TIMEOUT raised from 5s to 60s;
  new FSFS_INDEX_PROCESS_TIMEOUT of 120s for index commands. This
  addresses false failures on slow CI/rch workers (issue bd-1p1v).

- Timeout exit code fix: timed-out processes now return exit code 124
  (matching Unix timeout convention) instead of silently returning 0.
  A completion-marker heuristic checks stdout for "Discovered"/"Indexed"
  (index) or JSON with "ok" (search) before treating a timeout as
  success, preventing false passes when indexing is actually incomplete.

== Issue Tracker (.beads/issues.jsonl) ==

- Issue bd-1p1v updated from "open" to "in_progress", assigned to
  NavyHorizon, with investigation comment documenting the root cause
  (5s timeout masking partial indexing) and the fixes applied.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1omw","title":"Add fsfs search interaction latency hooks for frame responsiveness","description":"Parent: bd-2hz.9.5 (Optimize TUI frame pipeline and interaction responsiveness).\n\nImplement concrete latency hook coverage for high-frequency search interactions so frame-pipeline optimization has explicit measurable checkpoints.\n\nScope: crates/frankensearch-fsfs/src/adapters/tui.rs\n\nDeliverables:\n1) Extend TuiLatencyBudgetHook defaults with search-focused hooks for navigation and inline explainability updates.\n2) Use unique hook IDs + metric keys and explicit budget values aligned with existing global/search hook semantics.\n3) Add regression tests asserting new hooks are present and stable.\n4) Keep shell validation and existing tests green.\n\nAcceptance criteria:\n- Search interaction responsiveness has dedicated per-hook metrics in default shell model.\n- New hooks are deterministic and validated by tests.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GoldenElm","created_at":"2026-02-14T22:08:15.144781573Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T22:19:39.187382532Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T22:19:39.187356503Z","close_reason":"Completed search latency hook instrumentation slice with tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

-{"id":"bd-1p1v","title":"investigate: e2e_recall integration tests failing on rch workers (lexical index not created)","description":"Three e2e tests in crates/frankensearch-fsfs/tests/e2e_recall.rs fail on rch workers: e2e_index_search_verify_recall (lexical index directory not created), e2e_search_json_output_structural_contract, and e2e_search_empty_query_returns_no_results. The fsfs binary completes indexing (exit=0) but doesn't create the lexical subdirectory. May be related to FTS5/lexical feature availability in the rch worker environment.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-20T20:47:52.309489125Z","created_by":"BoldLynx","updated_at":"2026-02-20T20:47:52.309489125Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-1p1v","title":"investigate: e2e_recall integration tests failing on rch workers (lexical index not created)","description":"Three e2e tests in crates/frankensearch-fsfs/tests/e2e_recall.rs fail on rch workers: e2e_index_search_verify_recall (lexical index directory not created), e2e_search_json_output_structural_contract, and e2e_search_empty_query_returns_no_results. The fsfs binary completes indexing (exit=0) but doesn't create the lexical subdirectory. May be related to FTS5/lexical feature availability in the rch worker environment.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"NavyHorizon","created_at":"2026-02-20T20:47:52.309489125Z","created_by":"BoldLynx","updated_at":"2026-02-22T01:26:46.039193425Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":1624,"issue_id":"bd-1p1v","author":"NavyHorizon","text":"Claimed and investigated. Found test harness bug in e2e_recall: run_fsfs() kills process after 5s and returns exit code 0 on timeout, masking partial indexing as success and causing false lexical-dir-missing failures on slow rch workers. Patched timeout handling (30s budget, timeout returns 124) in crates/frankensearch-fsfs/tests/e2e_recall.rs. Also fixed compile blocker in crates/frankensearch-fsfs/src/runtime.rs for moved vector_writer (E0382). Remaining blocker: rch workers currently fail before fsfs tests due incompatible dirty /data/projects/frankensqlite state (63 errors in fsqlite-core).","created_at":"2026-02-22T01:26:46Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1p4b","title":"Test coverage: canonicalize.rs (frankensearch-core)","description":"Add 10 unit tests to canonicalize.rs covering: default config values, multiple code blocks, nested markdown, heading levels, language-tagged code blocks, http URL filtering, blank line preservation, query truncation, trait object safety, large document","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"LavenderIbis","created_at":"2026-02-15T06:14:00.149039925Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-15T06:15:03.520094711Z","closed_at":"2026-02-15T06:15:03.520067661Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1pfc","title":"Use frankenjax vmap semantics for automatic batch embedding vectorization","description":"When frankenjax matures, use its vmap (vectorized map) transform to automatically lift single-document embedding functions into optimally batched versions, eliminating manual batch-size tuning and improving indexing throughput.

PROBLEM STATEMENT:
frankensearch's Embedder trait defines embed(&Cx, &str) -> Vec<f32> for single documents. For bulk indexing (10K+ documents), calling embed() in a loop wastes ~95% of ML model inference overhead because ONNX/transformer setup cost is nearly fixed regardless of batch size. The current solution is manual batching (bd-i37 BatchCoalescer), which requires hand-tuning max_batch_size, max_wait_ms, and priority lanes.

WHAT VMAP PROVIDES:
frankenjax's vmap transform takes a function f: T -> U and produces f_batched: [T] -> [U] that:
1. Automatically infers optimal batch dimensions from the input
2. Manages memory layout for cache-efficient batch processing
3. Composes with other transforms (jit for fusion, grad if needed)
4. Handles remainders (last batch < batch_size) transparently

RELATIONSHIP TO bd-i37 (BATCH COALESCING) — COMPLEMENTARY, NOT REPLACEMENT:
- bd-i37 handles the CONCURRENCY problem: coalescing multiple concurrent callers' embed() requests into shared batches with deadline scheduling and priority lanes
- bd-1pfc handles the COMPUTATION problem: making the underlying model inference itself batch-efficient
- They compose: BatchCoalescer collects requests -> vmap-lifted embed processes them as an optimal batch
- Without frankenjax: BatchCoalescer calls embed() in a loop or uses a manually implemented embed_batch()
- With frankenjax: BatchCoalescer calls vmap(embed)(batch) which automatically optimizes the forward pass

CONCRETE INTEGRATION:
1. Extend BatchEmbedder extension trait (from bd-i37):
   - Auto-derive embed_batch via vmap: embed_batch = frankenjax::vmap(embed)
   - The vmap-lifted version handles padding, batching, and memory layout
   - Embedder impls that don't manually implement BatchEmbedder get it for free via vmap

2. Index building pipeline (frankensearch-index or frankensearch-embed):
   - Replace sequential for doc in docs { embed(doc) } with:
     let embed_batch = frankenjax::vmap(|doc| embedder.embed(cx, doc));
     let all_embeddings = embed_batch(documents);
   - frankenjax handles chunking into optimal batch sizes internally

3. Compose with jit (optional, future stretch goal):
   - frankenjax::jit(frankenjax::vmap(embed)) would fuse the batch operation
   - Depends on frankenjax jit maturity for transformer architectures

EXPECTED THROUGHPUT IMPROVEMENT:
- Current (sequential embed): 128ms/doc with MiniLM -> 1280s for 10K docs
- With manual batching (bd-i37, batch_size=32): ~140ms/32 docs -> ~44s for 10K docs (29x)
- With vmap (auto-optimal batching): comparable to manual but with zero tuning and better memory layout
- The win is NOT faster than manual batching — it's SIMPLER (no batch_size parameter) and COMPOSABLE (works with any embedder automatically)

FEATURE FLAG:
`vmap = ['dep:frankenjax']` in frankensearch-embed/Cargo.toml
- Off by default
- When enabled, BatchEmbedder gets a default vmap-based implementation
- When disabled, BatchEmbedder falls back to sequential embed() loop
- The `full` meta-feature should include it when frankenjax is mature

CRATE PLACEMENT:
- frankensearch-embed/src/vmap_batch.rs: vmap-based BatchEmbedder blanket impl
- Update frankensearch-embed/src/auto_detect.rs: wire vmap into EmbedderStack when feature enabled

ERROR HANDLING AND FALLBACK:
- If frankenjax::vmap panics or returns error on a batch: fall back to sequential embed() per doc
- Log WARN on first fallback with error context, suppress subsequent for 60s
- Never let vmap failure block indexing or search — vmap is an optimization, not a correctness requirement
- If vmap produces output of wrong dimension: reject entire batch, fall back to sequential, log ERROR

DEPENDENCY CHAIN:
- Hard dependency: bd-3un.3 (Embedder trait must exist)
- Soft dependency: bd-i37 (BatchCoalescer provides the concurrency layer that calls embed_batch)
- Soft dependency: bd-2ba5 (frankentorch) since vmap over frankentorch inference is the ideal composition — vmap over ONNX via fastembed may not work (ONNX Runtime has its own batching)","acceptance_criteria":"1. vmap-lifted embed produces identical embeddings to sequential embed (cosine similarity > 0.9999 per vector)
2. vmap-lifted embed_batch handles non-divisible batch sizes correctly (e.g., 10007 docs with implicit batch_size=32)
3. Indexing throughput with vmap >= throughput with manual batching (no regression)
4. Memory usage during batch indexing is bounded (peak RSS < 2x sequential for same corpus)
5. vmap composes correctly with BatchCoalescer (bd-i37) for concurrent search queries
6. All existing Embedder tests pass when embed_batch is auto-derived via vmap
7. Works with all three embedder backends: hash, model2vec, fastembed/frankentorch
8. Fallback to sequential works when vmap errors (no search/indexing failure)
9. Feature flag compiles correctly: with vmap, without vmap, with vmap+fastembed, with vmap+frankentorch","notes":"Deferred by backlog harmonization pass. Re-entry criteria: (1) Sprint-1 keystone program bd-1zxn closed, (2) release gate bd-ehuk closed, (3) proof-lane contract bd-bobf has at least one completed exemplar, and (4) concrete EV score >= 2.0 with measured hotspot evidence.","status":"deferred","priority":4,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:25:40.190395530Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T00:26:13.447403750Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["experimental","future","parking-lot","research"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1pfc","depends_on_id":"bd-3un.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:48:50.560843374Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":458,"issue_id":"bd-1pfc","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"RATIONALE BACKFILL: Added future-integration acceptance criteria so this backlog item stays actionable and test-driven once frankenjax vmap reaches maturity.","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:29:14Z"},{"id":725,"issue_id":"bd-1pfc","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"EVIDENCE CLAUSE ADDENDUM: when implemented, this bead requires unit tests for vmapped/scalar equivalence, integration tests for batch-coalescing behavior, and e2e indexing benchmarks with structured logging/diagnostic artifacts and replay commands.","created_at":"2026-02-14T00:26:08Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1pgj","title":"Fix 6 deep-review bugs: NaN training poison, cosine_error_bound, MRL rescore_dims, circuit breaker CAS races, path traversal, embed_batch validation","description":"Fixes across 4 crates found via deep code review: (1) quantization.rs NaN training guard (2) cosine_error_bound double /255 (3) mrl.rs rescore_dims>=search_dims (4) circuit_breaker.rs CAS for state transitions (5) runtime.rs path traversal (6) runtime.rs embed_batch length check","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-15T16:35:56.986134885Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-15T16:36:03.778440624Z","closed_at":"2026-02-15T16:36:03.778417671Z","close_reason":"Fixed: (1) NaN values now skipped in quantization training to prevent min/max corruption. (2) cosine_error_bound removed spurious /255 (scales already divided). (3) MRL effective_rescore_dims clamps to >=search_dims. (4) Circuit breaker Open->HalfOpen uses CAS, trip()/reset() use CAS, force_open/force_close use unconditional store. (5) resolve_paths validates canonical path stays within target_root. (6) embed_batch result length checked before zip. All 1536 tests pass (1 pre-existing flaky test excluded).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bugfix"]}
